step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
Clinical reasoning: Early surfactant administration—within 30 to 60 minutes of birth—is linked to reduced risks of chronic lung disease, air leaks, and neonatal mortality. Specifically, timely surfactant therapy lowers the incidence of bronchopulmonary dysplasia (BPD) and improves outcomes by minimizing lung injury related to ventilation and oxygen exposure. Delayed administration correlates with worse outcomes, especially in vulnerable preterm infants.
